title: RULES-2-KNULL (the MANDATORY kernel-specific-attribution analog of the rules-1 c5/MD-7 knull ablation, at TRAIN time) — does the rules-2 train-time internalisation result depend on the KERNEL rules source at all, i.e. does fine-tuning on a KNULL plain-dictionary-derived materialised closure (tbox-knull, PROPOSED-ASM-1400..1404) differ from fine-tuning on the kernel-engine-derived closure (rules-2 B2), matched corpus? LEG 1 ($0 CPU byte-equivalence, EXECUTED 2026-07-12): the two rules sources derive the BYTE-IDENTICAL training corpus on the closed rules-2 inventory — 21,780/21,780 examples surface-equal, family-2 13,020/13,020 at fraction 1.0, proof sidecars included (poc/rules-2/results/knull-analog-result.json sha afcf09e8...). LEG 2 (GPU fine-tune c5k-vs-B2) is therefore CONDITIONAL-VACUOUS: it would train byte-identical arms and is REGISTERED, NOT RUN. Consequence for the ASM-1138/1438 claim cap: the knull comparison now EXISTS and its answer is deflationary — on this closed kinship inventory the plain-dictionary source is train-time-indistinguishable from the kernel source, so NO rules-2 outcome (pass or fail) licenses a 'kernel-specific value' claim; the cap's operative language is carried into the rules-2 GO record verbatim.

analysis_plan_ref.anchor: Appendix C SSC.3 (knull analog wiring; GO finalisation under maintainer issue #24 decision (C))
analysis_plan_ref.note: sha recorded in the rules-2 GO record's prereg_doc pin (single source of truth; this sibling record is finalised in the same commit)

hypotheses: H-R2K-1 (leg 1, mechanical): the knull plain-dictionary TBox (same closed constraint grammar, same shared ABox vocabulary, fresh knull-profile URNs for TBox-introduced terms) derives a materialised training corpus surface-DIFFERENT from the kernel-derived rules-2 corpus. MEASURED FALSE at $0: fully surface-equivalent (fraction 1.0 on every family and kind; id sets identical; proof sidecars identical).

H-R2K-2 (leg 2, conditional GPU): IF leg 1 had found divergence, LoRA fine-tuning on the matched knull-derived corpus (arm c5k) would differ from B2 on S-out. UNEVALUABLE-BY-CONSTRUCTION given the leg-1 result: c5k's training bytes are B2's training bytes; the contrast is 0 identically. Registered so the attribution question has a permanent, citable answer rather than an open cap.

arms: leg 1 (EXECUTED, $0 CPU): poc/rules-2/knull_analog.py — regenerate the rules-2 training examples twice over the IDENTICAL deterministic component set with the PINNED twin engine: side A kernel TBox (certificate.TBOX_PINNED; regeneration cross-checked byte-equal against the pinned data/rules2-train corpus, fail-closed ERR_KERNEL_REGEN), side B knull TBox (poc/rules-1-knull/inputs/tbox-knull, dir digest pin-verified against rules-1-knull-cert). Compare per-id training surfaces (context, question, answer, options); proof sidecars reported separately.

leg 2 (REGISTERED, CONDITIONAL — currently VACUOUS): arm c5k = LoRA on the knull-derived corpus at the rules-2 pinned hyperparameters, scored on the identical rules-2 S-out entity surface against B2 (matched, 3 FT seeds, same analysis machinery). RUN CONDITION: leg-1 family-2 surface_equal_fraction < 1.0 on a regenerated result. With the landed fraction 1.0 the arms are byte-identical and the leg MUST NOT be run (spend with a guaranteed-null contrast).

dependent_vars.name: family2_surface_equal_fraction
dependent_vars.definition: fraction of shared family-2 example ids whose (context, question, answer, options) bytes are identical across the two rules sources

dependent_vars.name: c5k_minus_b2_sout
dependent_vars.definition: leg 2 only, conditional: paired S-out contrast at the rules-2 statistics (two-stage BCa)

endpoints.test: leg-1 mechanical equivalence readout (PROPOSED-ASM-1850): fraction 1.0 with identical id sets => the sources are train-time-indistinguishable on this inventory (leg 2 vacuous; deflationary attribution answer); fraction < 1.0 => leg 2 activates on the divergence-bearing item set (matched c5k-vs-B2). No statistics: the quantity is a deterministic byte comparison over the full population, not a sample.

verdict_rules_note: PASS here means 'the attribution question is answered mechanically' (either branch of the primary endpoint), never a hypothesis endorsement; the gate is the leg-1 pin/anchor bundle (kernel pins byte-exact, knull tbox digest match, kernel regeneration byte-equal to the pinned corpus).
kill_criterion_verbatim: KILL-none: leg 1 is a deterministic $0 diagnostic and cannot kill anything. The binding discipline is the LEG-2 RUN CONDITION: with family2 surface_equal_fraction = 1.0, launching the GPU leg is PROHIBITED (guaranteed-null contrast; the spend would be theatre). The ASM-1138/1438 claim-cap consequence binds regardless of any rules-2 outcome: 'engine-materialised entailments are internalisable' NEVER upgrades to 'kernel-specific value' on this inventory, because the plain-dictionary source derives the same entailments.
extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Scoped to THIS closed rules inventory and THIS kinship vertical exactly as the rules-2 GO record scopes it. The equivalence result is a property of the CLOSED CONSTRAINT GRAMMAR both TBoxes compile to (PROPOSED-ASM-1402: the knull compile targets the same inventory) — it does NOT say plain dictionaries replicate the kernel on any richer inventory, on NL-robust parsing, on coverage growth, or on any domain where the kernel's explication depth exceeds what a dictionary definition pins down. It also does not validate the kernel: it is a two-sided deflation (neither source is privileged at train time here).

budget.note: leg 1 executed at $0 (~4 min, 2 shared CPU cores, nice -10). Leg 2 carries NO budget: its run condition is false; activating it requires a record amendment with a fresh divergent leg-1 artifact + its own dry-plan.

pins.harness_manifest: leg 1 is repo-local CPU (no staged-bytes manifest); the executable surface is fully pinned by artifact_hashes below

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1 verbatim from the frozen rules-1 record (reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py)

model_revisions.note: leg 1 uses NO model; leg 2 (if ever activated) inherits the rules-2 GO record's pinned R1 revision verbatim

runner_constraints.hardware: leg 1: CPU-only, deterministic, repo-local; leg 2: PROHIBITED under the current leg-1 result
runner_constraints.roles: leg 1 authored + executed by the build role (fable-build-4, GO finalisation 2026-07-12, owner writer-4); reproduction is 'python3 poc/rules-2/knull_analog.py' and must reproduce the artifact byte-exactly (no timestamps, pinned seeds only). ASM block PROPOSED-ASM-1849..1852 in poc/rules-2/asm-go-1847-1859.json (EMITTED, not registered). NO feasibility conclusion is stated in this record.
runner_constraints.artifact_notes: knull-analog-result.json fields: pinned_corpus_anchor (kernel regeneration byte-equal to data/rules2-train, hash-anchored), id_set (21,780 shared, 0 either-side-only), family2_equivalence (13,020/13,020 surface-equal, 0 proof-sidecar divergent), family2_by_kind (chain/cover/typing all 1.0), family13_equivalence (8,760/8,760), counts ledgers for both sides (identical skip/exclusion profile; 0 knull-side builder aborts).

supersedes_note: This record RETIRES the 'c5 knull-sourced-closure fine-tune: NOT in this campaign' deferral row (ASM-1138 carried by PROPOSED-ASM-1438/MD-R2-8 in the rules-2 record): the mandatory analog now exists as a first-class sibling campaign with an EXECUTED leg. It does not supersede rules-1-knull-cert (the inference-time certificate leg), which remains its own record.
